summary: Cross-cutting verified review of all migrated Avalonia parts + per-tool wiring requirement map for Grammar/Lists/Notebook/Words
logs: Review: 11 parts; confirmed findings = 27, uncertain = 1. Wiring mapped for 4 tools.

confirmed.title: Enter inserts an empty paragraph instead of splitting at the caret (parity gap + inaccurate doc)
confirmed.evidence: Core parity gap CONFIRMED. FwStructuredTextField.cs:126-134: the Enter branch of structuralKeyDown calls editContext.TryInsertParagraph(field, index) with NO caret-position or end-of-text guard, so any Enter inserts (test EnterAtParagraph_StagesAnInsertAfterIt, StructuredTextFieldTests.cs:133-151, fires Enter on a non-empty paragraph without moving the caret to the end and still expects exactly one insert). FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2741-2748 ParagraphInsertSetters does live.InsertNewTextPara(pos, null) — an EMPTY paragraph. Legacy StTextSlice.cs:24-28 class doc states paragraphs support 'splitting and merging ... using the usual keyboard actions' (RootSite/StVc editor, StTextSlice.cs:33-34/281). So mid-text split is genuinely not implemented — a real behavioral gap. SCOPED DOWN: the finding's 'no // PARITY note' is literally true (grep PARITY in FwStructuredTextField.cs = no matches) BUT the simplification is NOT undocumented — sttext-editable-design.md:74-75 positively specifies 'Add paragraph: Enter at paragraph end ... TryInsertParagraph(after)' and sttext-test-research.md:23 (row B2) explicitly maps legacy 'Enter splits/inserts' to the simplified insert. The 'inaccurate doc' sub-claim is WEAK/partial: the inline comment FwStructuredTextField.cs:121-123 explicitly says 'StTextSlice's split-at-end behavior, simplified: we insert rather than split mid-text', which acknowledges the difference; only the class-doc one-liner (cs:25) loosely says 'Enter at a paragraph's end inserts' while the code has no end guard.

confirmed.title: Backspace-at-start does not merge a non-empty paragraph into the previous one
confirmed.evidence: Parity gap CONFIRMED. FwStructuredTextField.cs:136-146: the Back branch only fires TryDeleteParagraph when string.IsNullOrEmpty(box.Text) && box.CaretIndex == 0 && paragraphCount > 1; there is no code path that appends a non-empty paragraph's text/runs to the previous paragraph (no merge). Composer ParagraphDeleteSetters (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2752-2762) only RemoveAt(index) with a keep-one guard — no text merge either. Legacy StTextSlice.cs:24-28 doc confirms merge was supported via keyboard. Tests confirm only delete-empty exists (StructuredTextFieldTests.cs:154-194: BackspaceInEmptyParagraph_StagesADelete and OnlyParagraph_...BackspaceDoesNotDelete; no merge test). SCOPED DOWN like finding 1: no in-code // PARITY note (grep empty), but NOT silent — sttext-editable-design.md:76-77 specifies 'Delete paragraph: Backspace in an empty paragraph (when >1) ... TryDeleteParagraph(index)' and sttext-test-research.md:24 (row B3) explicitly names legacy 'Backspace-merge' and maps it to the simplified delete. So 'silently dropped with no documentation' is overstated; the design/test docs document the simplification, only the code-level PARITY marker is absent.

confirmed.title: Lexicon Lookup / Add to Lexicon not available on the Avalonia StText editor
confirmed.evidence: CONFIRMED. RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:110-118 (StructuredText case) constructs FwStructuredTextField passing only WritingSystemFocused, Save, Clipboard — it does NOT forward context.MenuRequested, whereas the default/FwMultiWsTextField case at cs:138-140 passes context.MenuRequested. FwStructuredTextField's constructor (cs:44-50) has no menuRequested parameter at all, so there is no path to surface an in-text context menu. The product view does supply it: LexicalEditRegionView.cs:586-594 BuildEditor builds RegionFieldControlContext with menuRequested: _menuRequested. Legacy affordances exist and are word-aware: StTextView.OnRightMouseUp shows mnuStTextChoices (StTextSlice.cs:355-366), carrying OnLexiconLookup (cs:95-113) and OnAddToLexicon (cs:171-203), both keyed off SelectionBeginningGrowToWord/GetWordLimitsOfSelection (cs:80-130) — a word-level affordance a field kebab cannot replicate. The row-level kebab (field.MenuId) is wired separately (LexicalEditRegionView.cs:409-429), so the gap is specifically the in-text Lexicon Lookup/Add to Lexicon. Parenthetical confirmed: FwStructuredTextField never sets/clears box.ContextFlyout (no occurrence in the file), unlike FwFieldControls.cs:158/175 which manages ContextFlyout, so the StText boxes keep Avalonia's default clipboard flyout. No // PARITY deferral note for this in FwStructuredTextField, and the §19a design's 'Deferred (named, not silent)' list (sttext-editable-design.md:132-139) covers only ORC paragraphs — Lexicon Lookup/Add to Lexicon is not listed.

confirmed.title: Spell check absent across the Avalonia surface (cross-cutting, not StText-specific)
confirmed.evidence: CONFIRMED as a cross-cutting absence, correctly framed. Legacy StTextView.Init sets DoSpellCheck = true (StTextSlice.cs:282). Grep for DoSpellCheck|SpellCheck|IsSpellCheck|spellcheck (case-insensitive) across Src/Common/FwAvalonia returned no files — no spell-check support on the Avalonia surface. The finding's framing is supported by the docs: spell check is a documented surface-wide / document-text-engine deferral, not StText-specific — opentype-font-features views-migration-matrix.md:409-545 schedules spellcheck overlays for Phase 4 after selection geometry/paragraph parity, and avalonia-end-game proposal/spec keep spelling behind retained non-UI service seams out of the Avalonia editor path. Severity low and 'no StText-specific change needed' is the appropriate disposition.

confirmedReview.part: 19b-features (Feature structures + MSA group box + feature dialogs)

confirmed.title: Inflection-feature sibling order not sorted alphabetically (parity delta vs legacy)
confirmed.evidence: Legacy: MsaInflectionFeatureListDlg.FinishLoading (MsaInflectionFeatureListDlg.cs:280-281) calls ExpandAll() then Sort(). FeatureStructureTreeView.Sort (FeatureStructureTreeView.cs:84-122) sorts each collection via list.Sort() using FeatureTreeNode.CompareTo (cs:429-435: by Text/display name) and recurses only into non-terminal levels (line 104-105 'sort all but terminal nodes'); value children stay in ValuesSorted/AddNode order (cs:144-147). Avalonia: BuildNodes iterates cur.InflectableFeatsRC in raw model order (FwFeatureStructureAdapter.cs:64), AddDefnNode iterates type.FeaturesRS in raw order (cs:106), NO sort; BuildPhonologicalNodes DOES OrderBy Name (cs:127), proving the omission is inflection-specific. RebuildTree is a pure depth-fold preserving _nodes order (FwFeatureStructureEditor.cs:408-445). Caller clean: LcmInflectionFeatureChooserLauncher.BuildInput->BuildNodes(pos) (cs:118)->Input.Nodes->Editor.SetNodes (FeatureChooserDialogViewModel.cs:46), no Sort/OrderBy. No test asserts sort order.

confirmed.title: Phonological feature values show full Name instead of Abbreviation
confirmed.evidence: Legacy PhonologicalFeatureChooserDlg.PopulateValuesCombo (PhonologicalFeatureChooserDlg.cs:400-405) uses val.Abbreviation.BestAnalysisAlternative ('+'/'-'). Avalonia BuildPhonologicalNodes emits values via ValueName (FwFeatureStructureAdapter.cs:131); ValueName returns val.Name?.BestAnalysisAlternative?.Text (cs:507-508), the full name. Caller clean: LcmPhonologicalFeatureChooserLauncher.BuildInput->BuildPhonologicalNodes (cs:101)->Input.Nodes->SetNodes (FeatureChooserDialogViewModel.cs:46). Finding's secondary claim correct: inflection legacy uses Name (FeatureStructureTreeView.cs:174), so single ValueName=Name helper is right for inflection but a visible delta for phonological.

confirmed.title: FwMsaGroupBox doc/PARITY comments misframe net-new inflection-feature & inflection-class widgets as parity scope cuts
confirmed.evidence: Legacy MSAGroupBox has only four widgets: m_tcMainPOS, m_tcSecondaryPOS, m_fwcbSlots, m_fwcbAffixTypes (MSAGroupBox.cs:44-50) plus MSAType; SandboxMSA getter sets only MsaType/MainPOS/SecondaryPOS/Slot (cs:66-101). MsaCreatorDlg hosts that plain box (MsaCreatorDlg.cs:37,152) returning only SandboxMSA (cs:56). FwMsaGroupBox.cs:533-534 attributes an 'Inflection Features affordance' to 'the legacy box' that the four-widget box never had -- the misframe. LcmMsaCreatorDialogLauncher.cs:299 itself admits 'SandboxGenericMSA carries no inflection-class field', so the MsaCreator inflection-class non-round-trip (cs:297-303) is an incomplete NEW capability, not a PARITY cut. The 572-574 comment ('legacy derivational MSA carries from/to ...') is accurate about the MSA model (IMoDerivAffMsa.FromMsFeaturesOA exists) and is NOT itself a misframe; confirmation rests on 533-534 + 299. No functional defect: Insert Entry / Add New Sense round-trip both (cs:296,302-303).

confirmed.title: Editor opens collapsed and selects nothing; legacy expanded all and selected the first node
confirmed.evidence: Legacy FinishLoading (MsaInflectionFeatureListDlg.cs:280-283): ExpandAll(), Sort(), SelectedNode=Nodes[0]. Avalonia ApplyAssignments calls ExpandAncestors only when chosen != null && !chosen.IsNone (FwFeatureStructureEditor.cs:489-490); RebuildTree seeds every closed feature's <None> radio as chosen (cs:447-457), so a fresh FS has all features IsNone, the 489 guard is false, ExpandAncestors never runs. No ExpandAll equivalent and no initial selection anywhere in SetNodes/RebuildTree/ApplyAssignments. Fresh FS renders fully collapsed, nothing selected; type-ahead filter mitigates. Not documented as a PARITY note.

confirmedReview.part: 19c-richtext: Rich-text depth (named styles / per-run WS / ORC)

confirmed.title: Rich-text-depth span gestures are a no-op in production: focusable affordance buttons steal focus and collapse the editor selection before the click handler reads it
confirmed.evidence: Reproduced via headless probe. Source: affordance buttons are plain focusable Button (RegionRichTextChrome.BuildSpanPicker FwStructuredTextField has no Focusable=false at RegionRichTextChrome.cs:96-107; BuildStyleAffordance styleButton FwStructuredTextField.cs:285; FwFieldControls.cs linkButton:637, deleteButton:713). Handlers read box.SelectionStart/End at click/open: FwStructuredTextField.cs:419-420 (onOpen) and :434-435 (commit fallback); FwFieldControls.cs:658-659 (link open), :732-733 (orc). FwOptionPicker.cs:150-151 sets list Focusable=false but trigger buttons are not guarded. Probe (single-run editable para, AvailableNamedStyles set, box visible+focused, sel 0,3): a REAL PointerPressed+PointerReleased on Discussion.Para.0.CharStyle drove box.IsFocused True->False and selection 0,3->0,0; flyout opened, picker committed 'Strong'; context.ParagraphTextEdits.Count=0 (NO style applied). Isolated blur probe: box.Focus() succeeded (IsFocused=True), blurTarget.Focus() -> IsFocused=False, selection 0,3->0,0 — Avalonia 11.3.17 collapses TextBox selection to caret on LostFocus. Control: same gesture via flyout.ShowAt()+CommitHighlighted() (no pointer) staged Count=1 styled='dog'. Affects all four gestures on both editors (shared box.SelectionStart/End-at-click pattern).

confirmed.title: Depth-picker tests route around the focus-collapse interaction, so the broken gesture stays green
confirmed.evidence: StructuredTextRichDepthTests.cs:99-108 (CharStyle) and :160-169 (WsRetag) set box.SelectionStart/End directly then call flyout.ShowAt(styleButton)/picker.OptionsList.SelectedIndex/picker.CommitHighlighted() — never a real PointerPressed/Click on the affordance button. RegionLinkOrcEditingTests.cs uses linkButton.RaiseEvent(new RoutedEventArgs(Button.ClickEvent))+flyout.ShowAt (e.g. :89-90, :127-128, :156-157, :189) — also no real pointer. My probe proved the production pointer path collapses the selection and stages nothing (Count=0) while these very paths stage 'do'/'dog' (Count=1). RaiseEvent(ClickEvent) and ShowAt() do not move focus off the TextBox, so the focus-collapse never fires in test — the suite asserts over a path that bypasses the production focus transition. Verdict tied to finding 1 (confirmed bug), so this masking is real, not a minor coverage nit.

confirmed.title: Hyperlink / ORC insert affordance is missing on the StText editor while present on the multistring editor
confirmed.evidence: FwStructuredTextField.BuildParagraphRow (FwStructuredTextField.cs:74-234) builds only styleAffordance/charStyleAffordance/wsAffordance/addAffordance/deleteAffordance (declared :99-103, assigned :116-171) — no Link or OrcDelete control. The multistring editor FwFieldControls.cs builds both: linkButton at :637-705 and orc deleteButton at :712-749. Algorithms exist and are real: ApplyHyperlink (LexicalEditRegionModel.cs:810), EditHyperlinkUrl (:842), DeleteOrcRun (:881). No '// PARITY' note in FwStructuredTextField.cs scopes out link/ORC (grep returned nothing). tasks.md 19c.3 (lexical-edit-avalonia-migration/tasks.md:377) says external links 'insert/edit/delete' with no StText carve-out; the only documented StText restriction (§19c.3, file header :32-34) is that an ORC-BEARING paragraph is read-only, which does not cover inserting a link into a clean editable prose paragraph. RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:110-116 routes RegionFieldKind.StructuredText to new FwStructuredTextField, so prose fields (Definition/Discussion/Comment) — exactly where hyperlinks belong — get the editor that lacks the affordance. Asymmetry is real and undocumented.

confirmed.title: Task 19d.2 is checked complete but claims in-cell browse picture editing that is not realized in product
confirmed.evidence: tasks.md (lexical-edit-avalonia-migration/tasks.md:382) marks 19d.2 [x] claiming picture insert/replace/delete/metadata 'in the detail view AND in-cell in browse', unqualified. In browse, ClerkBrowseEditContext.For (ClerkBrowseEditContext.cs:110) returns LexicalEditRegionEditContext, a sealed RegionEditContextBase subclass (LexicalEditRegionEditContext.cs:21) that does NOT override the picture methods (grep for TryInsertPicture/TryReplacePicture/TryDeletePicture in that file = 0 hits), so it inherits the rejecting base overrides (RegionEditContextBase.cs:75-90, all return false). Only ComposedRegionEditContext implements them (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:3506-3543). Decisive product fact: ClerkBrowseRowSource.GetEditField (ClerkBrowseRowSource.cs:549-578) UNCONDITIONALLY returns kind: RegionFieldKind.Text and never classifies an Image column (grep RegionFieldKind.Image in that file = 0 hits); no IRegionMediaServices is threaded through the browse host. So no browse picture cell is realized in product -- noted honestly at ClerkBrowseEditContext.cs:146-156 ('PARITY 19f.5 ... no picture cell is realized in product today; the view + edit-context lane are ready'). The detail-view path IS complete + tested (RegionPictureAndAudioEditingTests.cs:105-200 insert/replace/delete/metadata/ORC; PictureAndAudioFieldTests.cs:85-295). The 19d.2 [x] text over-claims: shipped product is detail-view only and the browse deferral lives under a DIFFERENT task (19f.5 code note), not reflected in the 19d.2 checkbox.

uncertain.title: Picture-properties dialog is an undocumented reduction of the WinForms original (crop/edit toolbox, Art-of-Reading gallery, and Copy/Move/Leave file-location choice all dropped)
uncertain.evidence: The REDUCTION reproduces cleanly: Avalonia VM (PicturePropertiesDialogViewModel.cs:23-27,79-85) carries only caption/description/license/creator + ImageFile; the View (PicturePropertiesDialogView.cs) has NO crop/gallery/move/leave/file-location UI (grep = 0 matches). The sole write path RegionPictureEditor (CreatePicture/ReplaceFile/InsertPictureOrc) always passes CmFolderTags.DefaultPictureFolder (lines 46,60,107), so Move and Leave/link are structurally impossible. WinForms original confirms dropped features: ImageToolbox crop/edit (PicturePropertiesDialog.cs:21,66-68), ImageGallery/Art of Reading (cs:22,64 m_isAOR), FileLocationChoice Copy/Move/Leave (cs:40,50; ApplyMoveCopyOrLeaveFile:374-393 via MoveOrCopyFilesController). Narrower claims hold: Src-wide // PARITY grep on toolbox/crop/gallery/FileLocation/Copy/Move/Leave found NONE (only LcmRegionMediaServices.cs:33,169 audio record + ClerkBrowseEditContext.cs:154 browse column). HOWEVER 'undocumented/silent drop' is OVERSTATED: openspec/changes/lexical-edit-avalonia-migration/media-pictures-test-research.md (the 19d T0 note) documents the legacy copy/move/leave + ImageToolbox capability (line 15) and specifies a deliberately metadata-only replacement (Design decisions, lines 33-55: 'the dialog captures all four'; only four edit-seam methods). So the reduction is documented by design-scoping/omission, not silently -- though not flagged with an explicit deferral line the way audio cross-platform record IS (lines 57-65 + // PARITY marker). Net: real parity gap, framing partly refuted; proposed documentation fix is harmless.

confirmed.title: FwGenDateField corrupts the year of any GenDate that has a month/day when a qualifier is edited
confirmed.evidence: Reproduced end-to-end by reflection probe against Output/Debug/SIL.LCModel.Core.dll. (1) new GenDate(Exact, month=6, day=15, year=1985, ad=true).ToLongString() => 'Saturday, June 15, 1985'. (2) Composer puts that exact string in the value slot: AddDateField sets display = gen.ToLongString() (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2590), gen read via get_GenDateProp (2584). (3) FwGenDateField.TryParseLongString (FwGenDateField.cs:222-234) seeds year from the FIRST run of digits; on 'Saturday, June 15, 1985' that run is '15' (reproduced: seed='15'). (4) Editing any qualifier fires Commit (precision/era handlers at FwGenDateField.cs:135-136), which composes from Year=15: Compose(15, Exact, AD) => 'AD 15' (FwGenDateField.cs:187-199). (5) GenDate.TryParse('AD 15') succeeds with Year=15 (reproduced), and the composer setter stores it (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2621-2623). A 1985 day-precise date silently becomes year 15. Mechanism is surface-independent: BuildDate routes EVERY editor='GenDate' slice to FwGenDateField unconditionally (RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:322-323). Reachable: DateOfBirth/DateOfDeath are editable editor='GenDate' slices (CmPossibilityParts.xml:257,265) and the legacy default value carries month/day (GenDateLauncher.cs:48 new GenDate(Exact, now.Month, now.Day, now.Year, true)). Test gap confirmed: every GenDate test uses month=0/day=0 year-only values (RegionDateEnumEditingTests.cs:85,90; FieldTypeVisualTests.cs:101,129,158), for which the first digit run IS the year, so the suite never exercises a month/day string. (Custom-field-on-entry routing through FwGenDateField inferred from the unconditional BuildDate route, not separately traced; the in-scope custom GenDate display at LexEntryParts.xml:411-412 is a read-only jtview <gendate format='short'> path.)

confirmed.title: GenDate editor drops month/day granularity vs legacy GenDateChooserDlg (parity regression)
confirmed.evidence: Legacy data-entry GenDate editing opens GenDateChooserDlg (GenDateLauncher.cs:38-62 HandleChooser) which exposes precision/month/day/year/era and builds new GenDate(precision, month, day, year, ad) (GenDateChooserDlg.cs:168-203, esp. 178-182 month/day combos). FwGenDateField offers only year + precision (Before/On/About/After) + era (FwGenDateField.cs:78-128); Compose emits only year (FwGenDateField.cs:187-199) — no month/day controls. So a user who set 'June 15, 1985' in legacy cannot reproduce it in Avalonia. No '// PARITY' note flags the omission: grep for PARITY in FwGenDateField.cs and the composer GenDate path returns nothing. tasks.md 19e.3 scopes the feature to 'year + precision Before/On/About/After + era AD/BC' but does not record month/day as an intentional deferral or note the corruption risk. NOTE: I do NOT confirm the sub-claim that the class comment 'asserting parity' is inaccurate — the comments (FwGenDateField.cs:37-44, 204-205) honestly describe the editor as 'year granularity, like the legacy chooser's year/precision/era controls' and never claim month/day parity; that sub-claim is overstated. Severity is calibratable: this capability gap shares the F1 root cause; the silent corruption (F1) is the genuine high-severity item, this gap reads closer to medium given the 19e.3 year-granularity scoping.

confirmed.title: jtview / embedded-view behavior is untested beyond classification
confirmed.evidence: WalkEmbeddedView (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:3135-3170) resolves node.TargetLayout, guards cycles via _visited (3144-3149), recurses children at depth+1 (3157-3158), and falls back to a read-only ShortName row for empty TargetLayout (3138-3142), cyclic nest (3147), and unresolved/empty compiled layout (3163). Tests touching the jtview/EmbeddedView concept stop at classification and import: EditorKindMapClassifyTests.cs:31,67 and RegionConsolidationTests.cs:114 assert editor-string->RegionEditorCategory.EmbeddedView; ViewDefinitionTests.cs:143-167 (Import_JtViewSlice_*) assert the IMPORTER captures TargetLayout ('PronInEntry'/'PublishPron'). The 'jtview' literals in ViewDefinitionOverrideEdgeCaseTests.cs:30,33 are the ViewDefinitionModel viewType parameter, not an editor='jtview' slice. No test invokes Compose on an actual jtview slice to verify nested fields are emitted at depth+1, that a cyclic nest degrades to ShortName without infinite recursion, or that an unresolved TargetLayout falls back to ShortName (grep for _visited/CompileForObjectWithOverrides in test dirs finds no composition test). Gap is the composition behavior specifically, not import.

confirmed.title: Legacy enumComboBox sideEffect / refreshDataTreeOnChange are silently dropped
confirmed.evidence: Confirmed as described, NON-ISSUE today (no functional regression). Legacy EnumComboSlice invokes a sideEffect reflection method (EnumComboSlice.cs:219-225) and optionally RefreshList on refreshDataTreeOnChange (228-230). XmlLayoutImporter and all of FwAvalonia capture neither attribute (grep 'sideEffect|refreshDataTreeOnChange' over Src/Common/FwAvalonia returns nothing), and WalkEnumCombo's setter calls _sda.SetInt / IntBoolPropertyConverter directly with no side-effect or refresh (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2300-2315). In-scope entry-editor enumComboBox slices carry neither attribute: IsAbstract, Adjacency, Direction in MorphologyParts.xml (lines 239, 2406/2415, 2843/2851) have no sideEffect/refreshDataTreeOnChange. The attributes are used only on out-of-scope surfaces: WFIParts.xml (sideEffect), CmPossibilityParts.xml (refreshDataTreeOnChange). LexSenseParts.xml:527 uses sideEffectMethod on a 'multistring' Gloss editor, not an enumComboBox, so it is unrelated. No defect in scope; a // PARITY note is the only suggested follow-up.

confirmed.title: Exact-date Time field is editable in Avalonia where legacy DateSlice was read-only (note, not a defect)
confirmed.evidence: Confirmed as described, intentional enhancement (NOT a defect). Legacy DateSlice is display-only: RichTextBox ReadOnly=true (BasicTypeSlices.cs:373) and shows 'Date/Time not set' when unset (428). Avalonia: a visibility='never' Time field stays a read-only row (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2528-2536), but an always-shown Time field becomes an editable Date row via AddDateField(...RegionDateKind.Date) (2539-2542, AddField isEditable:true at 2604). The factory renders an editable TextBox (IsReadOnly = !field.IsEditable) plus a CalendarDatePicker (RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:317-417). The parse-on-commit setter rejects unparseable input via DateTime.TryParse (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2632-2638) so bad input cannot corrupt the field. This is a deliberate editability enhancement, surfaced only so the difference is not mistaken for a regression.

confirmed.title: Data-row right-click menu shows no host commands in product (mnuBrowseView parity lost); task 19f.1 marked FULL is an overclaim
confirmed.evidence: Product wiring proven: RecordBrowseView constructs the row source as `new ClerkBrowseRowSource(Clerk, m_avaloniaColumnSource, Cache)` (RecordBrowseView.cs:397) and passes only that into the host. ClerkBrowseRowSource's class declaration (ClerkBrowseRowSource.cs:59-62) lists 13 capability interfaces but NOT IBrowseRowMenuSource. A repo-wide grep for `IBrowseRowMenuSource` returns the interface decl (LexicalBrowseView.cs:551), the view field/probe (`_rowMenuSource = rows as IBrowseRowMenuSource`, LexicalBrowseView.cs:621,799), and exactly ONE implementer: the TEST source `RemaindersSource` (LexicalBrowseRemaindersTests.cs:35). No product implementer exists. Therefore in product `_rowMenuSource` is null, BuildRowContextMenu's `_rowMenuSource?.GetRowCommands(rowIndex)` (LexicalBrowseView.cs:2502) yields null, and the menu adds only Cell Copy/Paste (LexicalBrowseView.cs:2526-2537). RecordBrowseView.OnAvaloniaRowCommandInvoked (RecordBrowseView.cs:889-898) can only fire if a command is chosen, which requires commands to exist — so it is dead in product. Parity-loss proof (the discriminating evidence, distinguishing this from RDE's genuine non-applicability): the legacy standard browse DID raise the rich row menu — RecordBrowseView.cs:290 `ui.HandleRightClick(m_mediator, m_propertyTable, sender, true, "mnuBrowseView")` (Delete Entry, Show in Concordance, etc.). Asymmetry: 19f.2 (RDE) wrote an explicit `// PARITY §19f.7` for its absent clerk-backed source AND ClerkBrowseRowSource's class comment documents the RDE deferral (lines 50-57), but 19f.1 (tasks.md:394) is marked `[x] ... (FULL): ... (host commands + universal Copy/Paste)` with NO PARITY note for the command-supply side, and the class comment never mentions a row-menu deferral. So FULL is an overclaim: the host-command half is unimplemented in product. CONTEXT for severity: 19f is the 'behind the shell, switchable' gated new path (tasks.md:393), not the default product surface — users hit this only in the new Avalonia browse.

confirmed.title: CSV export has no user-reachable trigger in the product browse
confirmed.evidence: The CSV computation is complete and the host bridge exists: BrowseCsvExporter.ToCsv (BrowseCsvExporter.cs:29, RFC-4180 quoting), LexicalBrowseView.ExportVisibleCsv (LexicalBrowseView.cs:1914), and the host hook LexicalBrowseHostControl.ExportVisibleCsv() (LexicalBrowseHostControl.cs:234). But a repo-wide grep for `ExportVisibleCsv` returns ONLY those two production members plus tests (LexicalBrowseRemaindersTests.cs:274,277) — zero product callers. RecordBrowseView has no Export/Csv reference at all (grep for `Export|Csv|CSV` in RecordBrowseView.cs: no matches), so no menu item, toolbar button, or command wires the host hook. Because the only bridge from any UI command into the new export is a C# call to `ExportVisibleCsv()` and there are no callers outside host/view/tests, no XML-defined menu/toolbar command can reach it either. tasks.md:398 marks 19f.5 Export `[x] (FULL bounded)`; its PARITY note defers only 'OS print + the full legacy ConfiguredExport', NOT the CSV trigger. browse-remainders-test-research.md:207 says it is 'surfaced via ExportVisibleCsv() + a host hook' — the hook exists but is not user-invokable. Scope: this is about the NEW ExportVisibleCsv trigger being unreachable; the legacy shell OnExport/ExportDialog path is out of scope and not asserted broken.

confirmed.title: No test that RTL/complex-script text survives CSV export un-mangled
confirmed.evidence: The three CSV tests use ASCII only: ExportVisibleCsv_RendersHeadersAndRows (LexicalBrowseRemaindersTests.cs:274-282, 'Lexeme Form,Gloss' / 'lexeme 0,gloss 0'), Csv_QuotesFieldsWithCommasQuotesAndNewlines (285-298, ASCII with embedded comma/quote/newline), Csv_EmptyRows_YieldsHeaderOnly (301-305). The RTL Hebrew test at ClerkBrowseRowSourceIntegrationTests.cs:1585 (ComputeReplaced_DiacriticInsensitive_RtlScript_NoSpuriousMatch, asserts ComputeReplaced("שלום", ...)) exercises the Find/Replace path, NOT the CSV path. No CSV test covers RTL/Hebrew/Arabic or combining marks. The finding's self-classification as a coverage nicety (not a defect) is correct: BrowseCsvExporter.Quote (BrowseCsvExporter.cs:53-60) triggers only on comma/quote/CR/LF, so RTL/complex-script content passes through byte-for-byte and cannot be mangled by the quoting logic.

confirmed.title: WS-props and SpecialCharacter dialogs are built + unit-tested but have zero product callers; tasks.md marks them done
confirmed.evidence: Grep for WritingSystemPropertiesDialogView/ViewModel and SpecialCharacterDialogView/ViewModel across Src (excluding tests) returns only the dialogs' own definitions plus two COMMENT lines in FwXWindow.cs (1552, 954) — no product caller, no Lcm*Launcher (Glob of Lcm*Launcher*.cs shows none for either). FwXWindow.cs:1543-1561 ShowWsPropsDialog unconditionally builds legacy FwWritingSystemSetupDlg; its own comment at 1553 admits 'this list-management call site is not yet gated onto it'. FwXWindow.cs:948-969 OnShowCharMap unconditionally shells charmap.exe/gucharmap (comment 952-956 calls SpecialCharacterDialog a NET-NEW picker for a 'future' insert affordance). Yet tasks.md:402 marks 19g.1 '[x] Avalonia managed (WritingSystemPropertiesDialog)' and tasks.md:405 marks 19g.4 SpecialCharacter '[x] migrated', with PARITY notes that defer only CONTENT (SLDR/converters/advanced tabs; reversal/occurrence pickers) — no wiring-deferral note. confirmed.title: Plain 'Go to lexical entry' (OnGotoLexEntry) is not gated to the Avalonia EntryGo dialog
confirmed.evidence: EntryDlgListener.cs:293-311 GoLinkEntryDlgListener.OnGotoLexEntry unconditionally does `using (var dlg = new EntryGoDlg())` and ShowDialog -> JumpToRecord, with NO UIMode/AvaloniaOptionsDialogLauncher gate. Contrast the two siblings in the SAME file: InsertEntryDlgListener.DialogInsertItemInVector gates at EntryDlgListener.cs:89-90 (`var uiMode = m_propertyTable.GetStringProperty("UIMode",null); if(AvaloniaOptionsDialogLauncher.ShouldUseAvaloniaOptionsDialog(uiMode))`) -> LcmInsertEntryDialogLauncher.Show (93-94); MergeEntryDlgListener.RunMergeEntryDialog gates identically at 185-186 -> LcmMergeEntryDialogLauncher.Show (189). The legacy WinForms path is fully intact (not deleted), consistent with a switchable phase. The finding's fix premise also holds: EntryGoDialogViewModel.cs:15-16 documents it as the kit replacement for the 'EntryGoDlg/BaseGoDlg family' (general WS-aware entry search), so the plain-search case is supported. Scope check: grep for GotoLexEntry/GoLink/'Go to'/FindLexicalEntry across *.md returned nothing; tasks.md (openspec/changes/lexical-edit-avalonia-migration/tasks.md:326) enumerates kit scope as 'InsertEntry ... EntryGo family + Merge + Link{EntryOrSense,Allomorph,MSA} (+ sense path), Chooser (flat/tree + shift-range)' with NO listing of plain GoTo/GotoLexEntry and NO explicit deferral note. So this is an undocumented-but-bounded deferral on the intact legacy path, exactly as the finding (low) characterizes it -- not a contradicted [x] claim and not a render/behavior drop.

confirmed.title: Chooser kit lacks legacy Any/All/None match modes and goto/dialog/create-new links
confirmed.evidence: Legacy affordances exist as cited: ReallySimpleListChooser.cs:597 ShowAnyAllNoneButtons(ListMatchOptions,...) building m_AnyButton/m_AllButton (608-614); the goto/dialog/simple link region at :895-924 (AddLink kGotoLink / kDialogLink / AddSimpleLink); guicontrol browse-view swap ReplaceTreeView at :927-931. ChooserDialogViewModel.cs (read in full) implements flat/tree select, search, shift-range (ToggleChecked rangeFromAnchor 283-315), ForbidEmpty OK-gate (384-388), and Help (137-138) -- but has NO Any/All/None ListMatchOptions and NO links region. Kit-wide absence grep hardens this: `ListMatchOptions|MatchMode|AnyChecked|AllChecked|MatchAny` over Src/Common/FwAvaloniaDialogs/ returns only FilterForDialogViewModel.MatchAnywhere (an unrelated string-position toggle), and `LinkType|AddLink|kGotoLink|kDialogLink|CreateNew` returns only POS/feature CreateNew* events on the FeatureChooser/AddNewSense path -- none in ChooserDialogViewModel. Wiring claim holds: ChooseFromList (LcmChooserDialogLauncher.cs:94) has ZERO call sites (grep of `ChooseFromList(` finds only the declaration); the wired ChooserDialogViewModel consumers (FeatureChooserDialogViewModel, LcmCreatePartOfSpeechLauncher, LcmInflectionFeatureChooserLauncher, LcmPhonologicalFeatureChooserLauncher, RecordBrowseView) plus FwChooserField (tasks.md:85 §6.3) do not use match-modes or links. dialogs-test-research.md chooser content is filter/options-focused (rows 5, B4-B6) with no Any/All/None or links coverage, confirming the affordances are absent from the research doc. NOT a runtime regression (finding concedes this). Two nuances vs the finding text: (a) the '54 SimpleListChooser call sites' figure is not reproducible -- I measured 18 `new SimpleListChooser/ReallySimpleListChooser` instantiations and 77 total references; the substance is unaffected; (b) 'undocumented scope gap' is more precisely 'scoped by omission at tasks.md:326 with no explicit PARITY deferral note' -- the chooser scope IS bounded in tasks.md, just not the missing affordances.

confirmed.title: Label-column width persists on every LayoutUpdated, not only on user drag
confirmed.evidence: LexicalEditRegionView.cs:113-118 subscribes grid.LayoutUpdated and unconditionally calls _labelColumnWidthChanged?.Invoke(w.Value) whenever col 0 Width.IsAbsolute && Value>0. Col 0 is always GridUnitType.Pixel (LexicalEditRegionView.cs:98), so the condition holds on essentially every layout pass after first arrange. Grep confirms the ONLY subscription is line 113 and there is NO GridSplitter.DragCompleted handler and NO last-written-value guard (grep for DragCompleted/lastWritten/_lastWidth returned nothing). _labelColumnWidthChanged is wired to PersistLabelColumnWidth (RecordEditView.cs:798), which runs PropertyTable.SetProperty (RecordEditView.cs:1319-1328) on each invoke. PropertyTable.SetPropertyInternal (PropertyTable.cs:533-557) has change-detection, but PersistLabelColumnWidth passes doBroadcastIfChanged=false (RecordEditView.cs:1326), so the only saved work is the broadcast; the invoke + ToString + SetProperty + SetPropertyPersistence call path still executes every layout pass. Finding correctly scopes this as redundant persistence churn, not a correctness defect; the proposed fix (DragCompleted or last-written guard) is appropriate. Severity low is accurate.

editSurface.evidence: The compile/walk ENGINE is genuinely class-general, not LexEntry-only: CompileForObject/CompileForClass (FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:3278-3347) take obj.ClassID, climb the class hierarchy for ANY class's 'detail' layout, and compile it; the whole Walk/WalkField/WalkGroup pipeline operates on ICmObject. The LexEntry coupling that IS real lives in exactly three seams: Compose's ILexEntry entry point + hardcoded 'LexEntry'/'Normal' (cs:117-146), the RecordEditView line-735 guard, and RegionEditContextBase. result.synthesis:
# FieldWorks Avalonia Phase-1 Readiness Synthesis

**Surface-status caveat (applies throughout):** The Avalonia browse/edit surface is gated "behind the shell, switchable" (tasks.md:393), not the default product surface. Every issue below is a **fix-before-default** defect, not a live-production hotfire. Severity reflects user impact *once the surface is switched on*.

---

## A. TOP CONFIRMED ISSUES TO FIX NOW (ordered by severity)

### A.1 — BEHAVIOR FIXES (real defects, user-visible)

**[HIGH] GenDate year corruption — silent data loss.** Part 19e. `FwGenDateField.TryParseLongString` (`FwGenDateField.cs:222-234`) seeds the year from the *first* digit run in the long string; for "Saturday, June 15, 1985" that run is "15". Editing any qualifier fires Commit which re-composes from `Year=15` (`FwGenDateField.cs:135-136,187-199`), and the composer stores it (`FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2621-2623`). A 1985 day-precise date silently becomes year 15. Reachable on editable GenDate slices (DateOfBirth/DateOfDeath, `CmPossibilityParts.xml:257,265`); routed unconditionally via `RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:322-323`.
**Fix:** Parse the year from the GenDate model, not a digit-scan of the localized long string — read `gen.Year` directly through the composer rather than re-parsing `ToLongString()`. Add a month/day-bearing test (all current tests use month=0/day=0, `RegionDateEnumEditingTests.cs:85,90`).

**[HIGH] Rich-text span gestures are a no-op in production.** Part 19c (finding 1). Affordance trigger buttons are plain focusable `Button`s (`RegionRichTextChrome.cs:96-107`; `FwStructuredTextField.cs:285`; `FwFieldControls.cs:637,713`). Clicking one blurs the editor; Avalonia 11.3.17 collapses the TextBox selection to caret on LostFocus, so the click handler reads an empty selection (`FwStructuredTextField.cs:419-420,434-435`; `FwFieldControls.cs:658-659,732-733`) and stages nothing. Affects all four gestures (named style / per-run WS / link / ORC-delete) on both editors.
**Fix:** Set `Focusable=false` on the trigger buttons, mirroring the working pattern at `FwOptionPicker.cs:150-151` (which guards its list but not its triggers). **Must be paired with A.3.1** — the tests route around this path, so the bug regresses silently otherwise.

**[HIGH→MEDIUM, gated-overclaim group] — three checklist-vs-reality defects on the gated path:**
- **Data-row right-click menu shows no host commands** (19f). `ClerkBrowseRowSource` does not implement `IBrowseRowMenuSource` (only the test `RemaindersSource` does, `LexicalBrowseRemaindersTests.cs:35`), so `_rowMenuSource` is null in product and `BuildRowContextMenu` emits only Copy/Paste (`LexicalBrowseView.cs:2502,2526-2537`). Legacy raised the rich `mnuBrowseView` (Delete Entry, Show in Concordance — `RecordBrowseView.cs:290`). tasks.md:394 marks 19f.1 `[x] (FULL)`. **Fix:** implement a product `IBrowseRowMenuSource` on `ClerkBrowseRowSource`, or correct the checklist + add a PARITY note (see E).
- **WS-props & SpecialCharacter dialogs have zero product callers** (19g). Built + headless-tested but unwired; `FwXWindow.cs:1543-1561` still builds legacy `FwWritingSystemSetupDlg`, comment at 1553 admits "not yet gated." tasks.md:402/405 mark `[x] managed/migrated`. **Fix:** wire the launchers, or change to `[~] DEFERRED` matching siblings 19g.2/.5.
- **DeleteConfirmation wired only for LexReference relation deletes** (19g). The standard delete paths (`RecordClerk.cs:1518`, `FdoUiCore.cs:1036`) build legacy `ConfirmDeleteObjectDlg` with no UIMode gate; only `LexReferenceMultiSlice.cs:906-911,985-990` route to the Avalonia launcher. tasks.md:404 claims it "pairs with the browse Delete tab + detail delete." **Fix:** gate the two standard delete paths, or scope the task text.

**[MEDIUM] Lexicon Lookup / Add to Lexicon missing on the StText editor** (19a). `RegionFieldControlFactory.cs:110-118` does not forward `context.MenuRequested` to `FwStructuredTextField` (the multistring case at 138-140 does); the constructor has no `menuRequested` param (`cs:44-50`). Legacy word-level affordances exist (`StTextSlice.cs:95-203,355-366`). **Fix:** thread `menuRequested` into `FwStructuredTextField`; surface in-text Lexicon Lookup/Add to Lexicon.

**[MEDIUM] Hyperlink / ORC insert affordance missing on the StText editor** (19c, finding 3). `FwStructuredTextField.BuildParagraphRow` builds no Link/OrcDelete control (`cs:74-234`); `FwFieldControls.cs:637-749` builds both. Algorithms exist (`LexicalEditRegionModel.cs:810,842,881`). Prose fields (Definition/Discussion/Comment) route to the editor lacking the affordance. **Fix:** add link/ORC affordances to `FwStructuredTextField`, reusing the existing model methods.

**[MEDIUM] GenDate editor drops month/day granularity** (19e). `FwGenDateField` offers only year+precision+era (`cs:78-128,187-199`); legacy `GenDateChooserDlg` exposed month/day (`GenDateChooserDlg.cs:168-203`). Shares root cause with A.1's HIGH. **Fix:** add month/day controls (closes both the capability gap and the corruption surface), OR scope as intentional and add a `// PARITY` note + corruption guard.

**[MEDIUM] Enter inserts an empty paragraph instead of splitting at caret** (19a). `FwStructuredTextField.cs:126-134` calls `TryInsertParagraph` with no caret/end-of-text guard; composer inserts an empty para (`FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:2741-2748`). Legacy supported mid-text split (`StTextSlice.cs:24-28`). **Documented** in design/test docs (sttext-editable-design.md:74-75) but no in-code marker. **Fix (if pursuing parity):** split runs at caret; otherwise add `// PARITY` + tighten the class-doc one-liner (`cs:25`).

**[MEDIUM] Backspace-at-start does not merge a non-empty paragraph** (19a). `FwStructuredTextField.cs:136-146` only deletes empty paragraphs; no merge path. Same documentation status as Enter. **Fix:** implement merge, or add `// PARITY`.

### A.2 — DOC / PARITY-NOTE FIXES (framing, not behavior)

- **19d.2 over-claims in-cell browse picture editing** (19d). Browse picture cells are not realized in product (`ClerkBrowseRowSource.GetEditField` always returns `RegionFieldKind.Text`, `cs:549-578`); only detail-view is shipped + tested. The deferral is honestly noted under a *different* task (19f.5 code note, `ClerkBrowseEditContext.cs:146-156`). **Fix:** qualify the 19d.2 `[x]` text to "detail-view only."
- **FwMsaGroupBox doc misframes net-new widgets as parity cuts** (19b). `FwMsaGroupBox.cs:533-534` attributes an "Inflection Features affordance" to the legacy four-widget box that never had it. No functional defect. **Fix:** correct the comment.
- **CSV export has no user-reachable trigger** (19f). `ExportVisibleCsv` (`LexicalBrowseView.cs:1914`, `LexicalBrowseHostControl.cs:234`) has zero product callers. **Fix:** add a menu/toolbar command, or note the trigger as deferred (current PARITY note defers only print + ConfiguredExport).
- StText Enter/Backspace and GenDate month/day all want in-code `// PARITY` markers (their simplifications are documented in design docs but lack the code marker the repo convention uses elsewhere).

### A.3 — TEST FIXES

- **A.3.1 [paired with rich-text HIGH] Depth-picker tests route around the focus-collapse** (19c, finding 2). Tests call `flyout.ShowAt()` / `RaiseEvent(ClickEvent)` / set `SelectionStart/End` directly (`StructuredTextRichDepthTests.cs:99-108,160-169`; `RegionLinkOrcEditingTests.cs:89-90` etc.) — never a real pointer gesture, so they stay green over a broken path. **Fix:** add a real `PointerPressed`+`PointerReleased` test asserting a style is staged; must land with the `Focusable=false` fix.
- **GenDate tests are year-only** — see A.1 HIGH; add a month/day-bearing case.

### A.4 — NOT IN THE FIX LIST (confirmed but not defects — do not rank)

Editable Time field (19e, intentional enhancement), enumComboBox sideEffect drop (19e, non-issue today, out-of-scope surfaces only), spell-check absence (19a, documented surface-wide Phase-4 deferral), RTL CSV coverage (19f, quoting can't mangle non-delimiter text), label-column persistence churn (region-core, redundant work not a correctness bug), editor-opens-collapsed (19b, mitigated by type-ahead, low). The **uncertain** picture-properties reduction (19d) is documented-by-design-scoping; keep it as a doc note, do not promote to a fix.

---

## B. CROSS-CUTTING THEMES

1. **`FwStructuredTextField` is systematically thinner than `FwFieldControls`.** The StText editor is missing both Lexicon Lookup/Add-to-Lexicon (19a) and Hyperlink/ORC affordances (19c) that the multistring editor has — and prose fields (where these belong) route to the thin editor. Treat as one capability-parity sweep on `FwStructuredTextField`.
2. **tasks.md `[x]` overclaims vs wiring reality.** 19d.2 (browse pictures), 19f.1 (host commands FULL), 19g.1/.4 (dialogs "migrated"), CSV trigger. Pattern: VM+view built and headless-tested, but no product caller/gate. Needs a checklist-audit pass (see E).
3. **Tests route around production interaction paths.** Rich-text focus-collapse (19c) and GenDate year-only (19e) both pass because the test bypasses the real gesture/data shape. Where a fix lands, add a test that exercises the production path.
4. **Simplifications documented in design docs but missing an in-code `// PARITY` marker.** StText Enter/Backspace, GenDate month/day. Repo convention elsewhere uses `// PARITY`; these rely on design/test-research docs only.
5. **The `ILexEntry`-bound seam is the single bridge to whole-tool migration** (Section C). The compile/walk engine is already class-general; only three seams hardcode LexEntry.

---

## C. WIRING PLAN — Grammar / Lists / Notebook / Words onto main Avalonia UI

### C.0 — SHARED COMPOSER GENERALIZATION (one work item; gates EVERY edit surface)

**Verified once, reused for all four tools.** The compile/walk engine (`CompileForObject`/`CompileForClass`, `FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:3289-3347`) keys on `obj.ClassID` and is already class-general. The LexEntry coupling lives in exactly three seams:
- `FullEntryRegionComposer.Compose(ILexEntry entry, ...)` + hardcoded `"LexEntry"/"Normal"` (`cs:117,124,146` — confirmed).
- `RecordEditView.ShowAvaloniaEntry` guard `if (!(obj is ILexEntry lexEntry)) { ShowMessage(EntryTypeUnsupported); return; }` (`cs:735-741` — confirmed) plus the `IsChangeWithinEntry` refresh predicate (`cs:683`).
- `RegionEditContextBase` ctor requires non-null `ILexEntry` (`cs:25-29` — confirmed), `Entry` typed `ILexEntry` (`cs:33`), and `Validate()` hardcodes `LexemeFormOA`/`CitationForm` → `LexemeFormRequired` (`cs:93-104`); undo/redo labels `UndoEditEntry`/`RedoEditEntry` (`cs:144-145`). `ComposedRegionEditContext` ctor forwards `ILexEntry` (`cs:3405-3417`).

**Shared tasks (do these FIRST — nothing else unblocks without them):**
- (L) Add `Compose(ICmObject obj, string layoutName, ...)` overload; parameterize the model's (class, layout) instead of `"LexEntry"/"Normal"`. Files: `FullEntryRegionComposer.cs:117-148,3405-3417`.
- (M) Generalize `RegionEditContextBase`/`ComposedRegionEditContext` off `ILexEntry` → `ICmObject`; make `Validate()` per-class/pluggable (CmPossibility validates on Name/Abbreviation, RnGenericRec/Grammar have their own or none); generalize undo/redo labels. Files: `RegionEditContextBase.cs:25-33,93-104,144-155`.
- (M) Replace the `RecordEditView:735` LexEntry-only guard and the `:683` refresh predicate with the current record's class. Files: `RecordEditView.cs:683,735-758`.

### C.1 — QUICK WINS (cheapest first): browse registry enablement

All browse surfaces are blocked primarily by the tool-name allowlist `SupportedAvaloniaBrowseToolNames = {lexiconEdit, lexiconBrowse}` (`LexicalEditSurfaceResolver.cs:42-46,108-127`). The `ClerkBrowseRowSource` row/column path is verified class-agnostic.
